channel.title: Comments for page "Joke SCPs"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Joke SCPs"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We have quite a few threads for this question:

  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1593284/how-to-post-a-joke-scp
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1149856/how-do-i-submit-a-joke-scp
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-5047031/how-to-upload-a-joke-scp
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-410608/posting-joke-scps
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2243689/how-do-i-make-a-joke-scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a question. How do I make a new page?

item.title: Re: Little help here?
item.content:encoded: No. You need to edit the page to input the number and title for the joke article.

item.title: Little help here?
item.content:encoded: Hey, guys. When posting a joke SCP, do I just add the tag 'joke' for it to show up here?

item.title: Re: Editing Joke SCP Hub
item.content:encoded:
Am I allowed to edit the Joke SCP hub to add my page to it

Yes. If the page reaches deletion threshold though, it will be deleted. A lot of authors don't title their new page in the list until they're certain that it will stay on the site (as in, not a negative rating from the start).

item.title: Editing Joke SCP Hub
item.content:encoded: Am I allowed to edit the Joke SCP hub to add my page to it or do I have to ask an administrator? Because I want to add a page but don't want to get my writing permissions revoked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OK. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe the author removed it when they decided they didn't like the site anymore.

To respect the absent, please continue this discussion via PM if you have further questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happened to SCP-1548-CU?

item.title: i tried, okay?
item.content:encoded: SCP-792-J I don't know what I was thinking, writing this. I did get some feedback and I'm willing to fail try.

item.title: Re: Do i have to create cute joke SCP on April fools (1st)
item.content:encoded:
No. The SCP-CU articles happened in 2014. There's no requirement stating that those are the only kind of articles that can be created on April 1, or that SCP-CU articles can only happen on April Fool's Day.

That said, most people probably won't know what an SCP-CU is, so if you wanted to write one, it'd need to be rather good.

item.title: Do i have to create cute joke SCP on April fools (1st)
item.content:encoded: Do i have to create the cute joke scps during April fools?

item.title: Re: I feel dumb
item.content:encoded:
Pick any title that isn't taken, and then post a link on this list.

That simple.

item.title: I feel dumb
item.content:encoded: So how do you post a joke SCP? I'm not planning on doing it anytime soon, but it would be nice to know how though for future reference.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Thank you.

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Agrimony, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

I was wondering if you had any suggestions for me to make it funnier.

Did you read through the comments in the corresponding discussion thread for your new post? That would be the place for people to comment on, since it's linked to the page you're talking about. Looking at the list of joke SCPs is an extra step of work for a potential reviewer to do.

I'm not asking for you to take it over for me or begging for the page not to be deleted, I just don't want to disappoint anyone. Kepiche?

If you haven't already, you should create a sandbox page for yourself. The drafts you finish writing there should be shown to the IRC chat and/or the drafts forums for feedback before you make a new page on the mainsite. You'd potentially catch a lot of mistakes in the editing phase by doing this.

item.title: Re: Inky
item.content:encoded: Also, considering the low rating it currently has, I was wondering if you had any suggestions for me to make it funnier. I'm not asking for you to take it over for me or begging for the page not to be deleted, I just don't want to disappoint anyone. Kepiche?

item.title: Inky
item.content:encoded: Hey guys, I just added "Inky." Hope you like it, I spent a long time thinking about what it should be, wording, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish they would be made in order so if there are new ones I could better see which ones they are

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that I have removed a link leading to the rickrolling video, an edit which was made by WAN Foundation.

WAN Foundation, this is a hub for -J articles. This is not a platform to throw actual memes (like a rickrolling video). Only links to actual articles on the site should be present.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
we're out of numbers WE NEED MORE NUMBERS

Take a look at the list. We're technically "missing" a ton of numbers. People pick joke SCP numbers without needing to adhere to the 'only take open slots' rule that the mainlist follows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: where r all of the numbers? we're out of numbers WE NEED MORE NUMBERS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like you already did so, but yes, feel free to remove broken links if you're sure the corresponding page no longer exists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-2015-J no longer exists. Is it acceptable for me to remove it from the list?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Send me a PM on how to post it when I get a good joke.

Nobody here is telepathic, so you aren't ever going to get that PM.

To post a -J article:

  • Write a draft in your sandbox.
  • Use the draft forum and/or the chat to get feedback.
  • Apply that feedback.
  • Wait until you have a few people at least saying they would upvote it.
  • Make a new page in the wiki by typing scp-wiki.net/SCP-SOMETHING-J into the address box, replacing something with an apropos witticism.
  • Copy your draft from the sandbox.
  • Click Edit on your new page and paste your draft. Then save.
  • Edit the J-List and add an inline link to the list [[[SCP-SOMETHING-J]]], where something is your previously mentioned witty name. Then add a title for your joke after the link (just like all the other ones already there).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I looked for the guide on how to post a joke SCP, but I couldn't find it. Send me a PM on how to post it when I get a good joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Either someone hasn't written it up, or someone's finger slipped when they linked the nonexistent SCP-017T-J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks I thought I needed some Kind of approval

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can contribute an -J article like any other SCP or Tale on this site, on your own free will and by making an article as per normal. Whether it sticks or not depends on popular vote, as denoted by the rating system (-10 and it's set for deletion vote… the usual protocols).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So can I Add a J- SCP like that ? Do i need permision ?

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Revision 502 has been reverted to Revision 501 for including a link and title to a joke SCP that was not made 40+ minutes after the initial edit. Please add your entry to the jokes list only after you create the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, there is. Here. Praise it 'till it runs dry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe It's One of the dumbest question you've ever Read but… Is there a way to upvote/downvote/comment SCP-1047-J?

Also: If someone asked This question before, I probably ignored that post because I'm writing/ reading on mobile Phone because My Laptop is broken!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did not find a link to this anywhere, so I'm just gonna leave this link to an epic -J worthy article here.

http://sovietcreepypasta.tumblr.com/post/24069630823/item-scp-173-object-class-euclid-special

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what do I press to write a joke scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would you want to? It's perfectly styled as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Guys, can I change "The Troll" from a link that goes to the joke page into plain dark red rext instead? I found out that #B20000 is the same colour used by the wiki, it should like this:
"The Troll"

+ show block
– hide block

It looks the same, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, what happened to SCP-909-J?

Edit: nevermind. It's gone now.

item.title: Re: Bad Applications
item.content:encoded: Awww okay, Thanks for the help!

item.title: Re: Bad Applications
item.content:encoded: Just poking in to mention that Dr. Bright was also the page's author - thus, nothing horribly inappropriate happened.

item.title: Re: Bad Applications
item.content:encoded: It was deleted by Dr Bright, because the spirit of the page was excessively mean.

item.title: Bad Applications
item.content:encoded: There used to be an amusing page about the bad applications that people sent. It used to be under the link "http://www.scp-wiki.net/badapplications" But I can't find it any more ): Has it been moved/deleted?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could upvote 1047. But it runs away from me…

Edit: I found it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your frustrations are my upvotes.

bows

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamit TroyL. I clicked for 5 minutes until I got what was going on.

item.title: Re: Lunactic question
item.content:encoded: As far as I know, someone has yet to write that up, but if it's rated above -10, it can stay on the site.

item.title: Lunactic question
item.content:encoded:
Can we make Joke SCPs of Joke SCPs?
Yes, this is an actual question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much seconding this. I've forgotten what SCP-J he posted in, but I remember that he said almost exactly:

DOG FACT: Joke SCPs take 10 minutes or under to write.

—(No, it's not under SCP-1994-J, I've checked the whole thread.)—
But since I didn't know he said it as a sarcastic expression, well, now at least I know.

EDIT: My mistake, It IS in fact in page 2 of SCP-1994-J forums, except ObserverSeptember said this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sal said that. It was meant to reference how many -J SCPs need to be short and clever, and writing them for too long tends to make people overthink things. So, although it is not a fast rule, it is just a word of wisdom from the king of joke articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't recall it ever existing. In either case, Joke SCPs take as much effort and finesse to write as mainlisters. Usually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Joke SCPs are generally considered harder to write than main-series SCPs and are one of the top three most difficult things for new authors to write about (along with Humanoids and Keter-class objects).

So, I guess the statement "Joke SCPs take under 10 minutes to write" no longer exists anymore, or am I getting the wrong idea?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The History section indicates it was Aelanna. Yay Aelanna!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, you remade the -J page. Nice job, whoever that was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't believe so. This is a discussion page, not a forum thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would the comment I am currently posting be considered thread necromancy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And nothing of value was lost…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this conversation is translating to

WARNING GRAMMER DEACTIVATED
stop making terrible joke scp ideas and gtfo of our website

my reaction

your mom

I quit wikidot ZACNOTZACH AWAY!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
its zac and its ok I might create a page but who says its going to be down voted?

The people in this discussion thread who are telling you they'd downvote it if you posted it, I would imagine.

its a rickroll its funny!

Humor is subjective. The aforementioned site members do not find it funny.

either that or I write an SCP about a guy who has no idea what minecraft is

*facepalm*

Joke articles are not a dump box for crap that doesn't live up to normal article standards. Jokes are, in fact, harder to write well than standard SCP articles because of the humor factor; not everyone finds the same things funny, and in this case, we aren't twelve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you want to continue debating whether or not to create a page with this concept, take it to the Forums for feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Boyo. You've not only been advised by a moderator that this is a bad idea, but also an administrator. Those both a probably good signs that you should not go through with this idea.

As for your new concept, I personally can't see what's funny about that.
